creator: Jagmeet Singh
title: (No AI) As Anthropic suspends access to new models, India debates its AI future
dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
content: Tech leaders debate whether the Anthropic episode is a wake-up call for India’s AI ambitions.
contentSnippet: Tech leaders debate whether the Anthropic episode is a wake-up call for India’s AI ambitions.

creator: Kate Park
title: (No AI) Meta reportedly moves to unwind $2B Manus deal after Beijing’s demand
dc:creator: Kate Park
content: Meta starts dismantling its $2 billion Manus acquisition after Beijing ordered the deal reversed.
contentSnippet: Meta starts dismantling its $2 billion Manus acquisition after Beijing ordered the deal reversed.

title: (No AI) Amazon security research reportedly led to the White House’s Anthropic Fable ban
author: Terrence O’Brien
content:
![Anthropic logo on an orange and grey background.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/01/STK269_ANTHROPIC_2_A.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

According to the _[Wall Street Journal](https://www.wsj.com/tech/ai/amazon-ceos-talks-with-u-s-officials-triggered-crackdown-on-anthropic-models-dcc90578?mod=hp_lead_pos1)_, the export control directive that led to Anthropic [cutting off access](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/949553/anthropic-fable-5-mythos-5-government-national-security) to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 was triggered in part by cybersecurity research from Amazon and conversations between CEO Andy Jassy and the White House. According to the report, the paper from Amazon claims that, through a series of prompts, it was able to get [Fable 5](https://www.theverge.com/news/946725/anthropic-releases-claude-fable-5-mythos) to serve up information that could be used in cyberattacks. Amazon has yet to respond to a request for comment.

Shortly after Jassy shared the company's findings with the government, it made the call to block its use by foreign nationals. Complicating this issue is that many of …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/949601/amazon-anthropic-fablemythos-government-ban)
contentSnippet:
According to the Wall Street Journal, the export control directive that led to Anthropic cutting off access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 was triggered in part by cybersecurity research from Amazon and conversations between CEO Andy Jassy and the White House. According to the report, the paper from Amazon claims that, through a series of prompts, it was able to get Fable 5 to serve up information that could be used in cyberattacks. Amazon has yet to respond to a request for comment.
Shortly after Jassy shared the company's findings with the government, it made the call to block its use by foreign nationals. Complicating this issue is that many of …
Read the full story at The Verge.

According to the report, the paper from Amazon claims that, through a series […]

title: (No AI) My yard is dying, so I made an app for that
author: Allison Johnson
content:
![Image of a backyard with code brackets and a grill.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/268530_SUMMER_UPGRADE_WEEK_VIBECODING_BACKYARD_ADemidova.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

When I returned to my computer five minutes after giving Gemini a lengthy prompt, I had two things: a functional app in a preview window, and a message about a bug.

"~ Channel is unrecoverably broken and will be disposed!" Sounded bad! But right below it was a button to fix the bug. Pretty weird that I just instructed a computer to build a whole app for me with a single prompt, but it needed me to click a button to fix a bug. I did anyway, and in 233 seconds Gemini reported back that it had succeeded, using words like "blockages" and "race conditions." I didn't understand a bit of it. It was thrilling.

This was my second or third attempt a …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/942119/vibecoding-backyard-app-gardening-organizing)

title: (No AI) New OpenAI Academy courses for the next era of work
content: OpenAI introduces three Academy courses that help people build practical AI skills, create repeatable workflows, and apply agents in everyday work.
contentSnippet: OpenAI introduces three Academy courses that help people build practical AI skills, create repeatable workflows, and apply agents in everyday work.

title: (No AI) How Preply combines AI and human tutors to personalize learning

title: (No AI) Investing in multi-agent AI safety research

contentSnippet: Google DeepMind and partners announce a $10M funding call for multi-agent safety research.

creator: Fernanda González
title: (No AI) A Court Has Ruled That Google Is Liable for False Statements Generated by AI Overviews
dc:creator: Fernanda González
content: The ruling holds that a company that designs, trains, operates, and manages an AI system must assume legal liability for any damages caused by the responses it generates.
contentSnippet: The ruling holds that a company that designs, trains, operates, and manages an AI system must assume legal liability for any damages caused by the responses it generates.

creator: Maxwell Zeff
title: (No AI) Anthropic Says It’s Taking Claude Fable 5 Offline to Comply With US Government Order
dc:creator: Maxwell Zeff
content: “The government believes it has become aware of a method of bypassing, or ‘jailbreaking’ Fable 5,” the company said in a blog post.
contentSnippet: “The government believes it has become aware of a method of bypassing, or ‘jailbreaking’ Fable 5,” the company said in a blog post.
